Okay so this is how I handle a citation appeal. People submit them online through Driverseat, that's the parking portal, and they land in AIMS in a queue called Pending Adjudications. So first thing, I log into AIMS and open up Pending Adjudications. That's either off the top menu under Adjudications or there's a widget on the dashboard. One thing, if an appeal's got a check mark next to it, that means we're still waiting on the customer to send extra info, so I leave those, they're not ready to look at yet.

So I find the appeal I'm working and I click View Ticket, and that pulls up the actual citation, the original one. So now I can see the violation, where it was, the date, whose account it's on.

Then I verify. I'm checking the name, the license plate, the photo attachments if there are any, and the violation itself, against whatever evidence is on there. Here's the thing, if the plate on the citation doesn't match the plate in the photos, that's an automatic dismiss. I don't even review the rest, I just dismiss it, note the reason was the plate didn't match, and I jump straight ahead to the letter part. Otherwise I carry on normally.

Assuming it's fine, I click Adjudication from inside the citation, and that opens up the customer's side, the appeal reason they submitted and anything they uploaded. Then I click View Adjudication and that's the full record, that's where I actually enter the decision.

Before I decide though, I check the account. If it's staff I'll confirm their GT email's on the account. And sometimes a person has two accounts, like a temporary one that starts with GT and then a bunch of digits, and a permanent one that's just their GTID with no prefix. If there's duplicate accounts like that I have to merge them first before I do anything. I don't do the merge here really, there's a whole separate process for that, I just know I have to go do it.

Then the decision. There's three buttons. Uphold means I'm denying it, the citation stands, they owe the full fine. Dismiss is I'm approving the whole thing, citation comes off, fine's gone. And Reduce is partial, I knock the fine down but not more than fifty percent of the original, so capped at 50%. Whichever one I pick, I have to type a justification in the comments, that's required every single time, no decision without a written reason. How I decide between them is, well, it's the PTS rules and the officer's reason and what the customer's saying, you kind of weigh it, there's no set written criteria for where reduce ends and dismiss begins, you just weigh it. There's a list of reasons that are automatic uphold too, like "didn't see the sign" or "couldn't find a spot," those just get denied.

After the decision I click Delivery Options and pick Citation Appeal Decision from the dropdown, that's the standard letter, same one for all three outcomes.

Then Save. Now if I picked Reduce, an Adjustment page pops up automatically, and I put the reduction amount in the Adjudicate field, again it can't be over 50% of the original, and Save again, and AIMS handles the actual money side itself. If it was Dismiss or Uphold there's no adjustment page, it goes straight to the letter.

The letter populates with everything, the name, citation number, the violation, the tag, the decision, my note, amount due. I read through it, make sure it's all right, and then Send at the bottom. That emails it to them and the status in the queue updates by itself, and that's it, done.

Couple of other things that come up. Late ones, technically they've got ten days from the citation date to appeal, after that it's delinquent and there's a five dollar late fee tacked on. But if someone's late and they've got good documentation we can still take it, it's case-by-case, no firm rule on what counts as good enough documentation. Second appeals, students don't get one, first decision's final. Faculty and staff can do a second level but only if they've got new information, and that goes to the support email, not me. If there's a name mismatch, the name on the appeal not matching the account I don't process it, I send them an additional info request and put it on hold, wait for them to come back, up to ten days. And if the system's down, AIMS just unavailable, I tell the person sorry, come back next business day. Timing wise we try to turn these around in about three days but the official max is fourteen, and that whole SLA can stretch around Institute holidays.
